name: Fish Aspic
recipeIngredient: 1 large firm fleshed fish (white-fish, Canadian Sole, Flounder), about 2 pounds
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the whole fish and fish heads in cold water.
2. Run a knife over the fish, scraping from tail to head to remove any leftover scales.
3. Rinch the gut cavity.
4. Cut off the head at the gills, rinse again, and cut into equal pieces.
5. Rub inside and out with salt and set aside.
6. Remove the gills from the heads.
7. Combine the fish heads, tail, fins, and all ingredients in a 4-quart pot, then cover with 6 cups of cold water.
8. Bring to a boil, skimming off foam, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
9. Cook for 20 minutes or until the heads are cooked through.
10. Using a small sieve lined with a wet towel or cheesecloth, pour the liquid into a 2-quart jar.
11. When most of the liquid is poured off, press lightly on the remains and pour off the rest.
12. Place fish sections in about 2 cups of stock. Bring to a gentle boil and simmer for about 10 minutes until the fish turns white.
13. Do not overcook or the pieces will fall apart.
14. Using a slotted spoon, remove the fish pieces and place on a platter to cool.
15. When cool enough to handle, remove the skin and bones, keeping the pieces large and not ragged.
16. Cover and cool.
17. Strain the cooking liquid again and add to the rest of the stock.
18. For a more intense flavor, reheat the stock slightly and reduce by one-third.
19. Dissolve the gelatin in 1 cup of cooled fish stock and add.
20. Taste and adjust salt if needed.
21. Pour into enough stock to cover the bottom of a 6-cup fish mold or glass pan by 1/2 inch.
22. Refrigerate until set.
23. Remove the peel from cooked carrots and slice them into thin rounds.
24. Arrange carrot rounds in an attractive pattern on the aspic, add a little more cooled stock, and cool until set.
25. Evenly spread fish pieces over the aspic, covering with more stock, then cool until set.
26. Continue to add stock until the fish is completely covered.
27. Refrigerate overnight.
To Serve:
Run a sharp knife around the edge of the mold. Place a platter on top and invert (a hot damp cloth helps release it). Garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon slices just before serving.
